operating_state: Operating revenue decreased 60% to $4.2 billion compared to March 2019 quarter due to reduced demand from COVID-19 pandemic. The company reported a pre-tax loss of $1.5 billion, which included a $1.2 billion benefit from the first payroll support program extension grant recognition. Management noted improvement during the March 2021 quarter with expectations for continued recovery throughout 2021.
cost_structure: Total operating expense decreased $3.9 billion or 41% compared to March 2019 quarter, primarily from lower volume-related expenses including fuel and passenger commissions, recognition of payroll support program grants, and significant cost reduction measures across all operations. Operating expense on an adjusted basis decreased $3.1 billion or 33% compared to March 2019 quarter.
material_events: Delta entered into a $2.9 billion Payroll Support Program Extension Agreement with Treasury in January 2021, consisting of $2.0 billion in grants and $830 million in unsecured 10-year loans at 1.00% interest for five years. The company issued warrants to Treasury for approximately 2.1 million shares at $39.73 exercise price with five-year terms. Delta repaid $1.8 billion in debt and finance leases during the quarter, including $1.5 billion early repayment of slot-secured term loans.
macro_exposures: The company remains significantly exposed to COVID-19 pandemic impacts on travel demand and operational capacity. Delta faces restrictions on share repurchases and dividends through March 31, 2022, and executive compensation limitations through October 1, 2022 as conditions of government support.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued business improvement throughout 2021 following March quarter recovery trends. The company committed to $1.0 billion investment over ten years for carbon neutrality achievement, including $30 million planned for 2021 to address 13 million metric tons of carbon emissions through offset credits. Delta must maintain essential air service as directed by DOT until March 1, 2022.
uncertainty_profile: The company maintains liquidity of $16.6 billion including cash, short-term investments, and available credit facilities. Delta generated $710 million in free cash flow during the quarter supported by $2.0 billion from payroll support grants. No material changes were reported to previously disclosed risk factors from the 2020 Form 10-K.
